title: Create durable advanced program packets for AMP166 through AMP200
persona: IT, data, marketing, sales, product, engineering, SRE, and security operations leaders
steps: Open representative AMP166-AMP200 workspaces from the generated Assistant subnav.
Review live source signals, risks, recommendations, artifacts, approval routes, and rollback controls.
expected: Each AMP166-AMP200 workspace persists an AssistantAdvancedProgramPacket row with a distinct ampNumber and programKey.
Each packet has a domain-specific nav label, title, artifact, approval owners, and no-mutation scope.
The shared advanced-program UI shows preview metrics, historical packets, and queue-review controls.

No license, device, identity, SaaS, data catalog, content, campaign, lead, compensation, rev-rec, quote, support, roadmap, experiment, telemetry, release, SRE, vulnerability, patch, or EDR records are mutated automatically.

title: Queue human review for AMP166 through AMP200 packets
steps: Create or select a DRAFT packet.
Add a review note explaining the downstream action under consideration.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and records actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
AssistantActionQueueItem uses an AMP-specific actionKind such as assistant_amp166_review or assistant_amp200_review.
AssistantAuditEvent states that downstream records were not changed automatically.

title: Governance guardrails stay explicit across IT, GTM, product, engineering, and security packets
steps: Open a packet with active risk signals.
Inspect recommendationJson, approvalPlanJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson.
Queue review without executing downstream changes.
expected: recommendationJson requires review before operational, commercial, engineering, or security execution.
approvalPlanJson names the responsible owner groups.
rollbackPlanJson preserves source records and requires a fresh packet when evidence changes materially.
